note.current_position: Student is working on factoring basic trinomials (leading coefficient 1). Arrived at the correct factor pair for x²−5x+6 but has not yet verified by expanding.
struggle_points: wanted the answer supplied rather than working through the reasoning
has not yet confirmed factors by multiplying back out
what_worked: redirecting with a targeted question (what multiplies to c and adds to b) got the student moving immediately
note.last_session_summary: Last time we started factoring x²−5x+6 — student identified (x−2)(x−3) but the session ended before verifying by expansion.

topics_covered: factoring quadratics with a≠1 (AC method, splitting the middle term)
note.current_position: Introduced the AC method for a≠1 quadratics; can identify the correct pair (multiply to ac, add to b) but has not yet completed the grouping step.
struggle_points: grouping after splitting the middle term has not clicked yet
initially reached for the answer before trying the method
what_worked: 'Okay, convince me' pushed them to justify each step out loud
framing the task as finding two numbers
redirecting to the specific line that broke down ('factor just the first pair') to isolate the sticking point
note.last_session_summary: Last time we started the AC method on 2x^2+7x+3 — they found the pair 6 and 1 and split the middle term correctly, but got stuck factoring by grouping before we could finish.

topics_covered: completing the square for x² + bx + c form
identifying vertex form from completed square
note.current_position: Student completed one completing-the-square problem correctly and reached vertex form. Has not yet connected vertex form to reading off the vertex coordinates explicitly.
struggle_points: narrated steps correctly but may not yet own the connection between (x+3)²−4 and the vertex as a coordinate pair
what_worked: step-by-step prompting ('where does it go?') kept momentum going
affirming a clean step before moving forward helped student stay on task
note.last_session_summary: Last time we worked through completing the square on x²+6x+5, arriving at (x+3)²−4, and were just beginning to interpret that as vertex form.

topics_covered: quadratic formula and when to use it vs. factoring
factoring with a leading coefficient other than 1
note.current_position: Just introduced the quadratic formula; can identify a, b, c and evaluate the discriminant correctly, including sign handling when c is negative. Beginning to develop judgment about when to factor vs. use the formula.
struggle_points: sometimes forgets to subtract the added constant back off when completing the square
mixes up the sign of the vertex x-coordinate when reading it off (x+3)^2
reaches for the answer under time pressure before trying a method
hesitant to split the middle term when the leading coefficient is not 1
what_worked: 'Okay, convince me' gets them to justify each step out loud
asking which line they trust least to locate the error themselves
posing a choice between two methods to surface their own reasoning about when each applies
restating the rule as a question rather than telling them
note.last_session_summary: Last time we introduced the quadratic formula, identified a/b/c for x^2+x-1, and they correctly justified why the discriminant is 5 including the sign flip from a negative c.
